title: Pentacalia marinii
taxonomy: Pentacalia marinii belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family contains over 23,000 species, including many common garden plants and wildflowers. The genus Pentacalia, to which Pentacalia marinii belongs, is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Pentacalia marinii is a species of flowering plant that is endemic to the coastal regions of Central America. It is a herbaceous plant that produces small, white flowers.

intro: Pentacalia marinii is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to Ecuador's Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential antiparasitic and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Pentacalia marinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the most common preparations being used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
